pr.title: Splitting of Dolphin SDK, remaining CW MSL C code, and part of remaining Sysdolphin
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly organization PR that split monolithic Dolphin SDK and Sysdolphin assembly blobs into many subsystem-named asm files, split remaining CW MSL C code, extracted AXDriver and psdisp regions, and updated ldscript.lcf to preserve link order. The PR was primarily about file/module boundary recovery rather than C decompilation. The body documents a key linker-order pitfall: the linker partially matches object filenames, so names such as dsp_debug and string had to be shortened to dsp_dbg and strng to avoid collisions or unintended placement.
what_changed: Removed the previous monolithic asm/dolphin_sdk.s assembly blob, deleting 57377 lines.
Removed the previous monolithic asm/code_8038838C.s blob, deleting 53529 lines.

Added many smaller assembly files named after likely SDK, MSL, and Sysdolphin object/module boundaries, including AX.s, AXDriver.s, CARD.s, GXFifo.s, GXInit.s, dvdlow.s, dvdqueue.s, dsp.s, dsp_dbg.s, dsp_task.s, jpegdec.s, psdisp.s, strng.s, strtoul.s, and others.

Created large residual split files asm/code_803A3920.s, asm/code_8038FAA8.s, asm/dolphin_sdk_1.s, and asm/dolphin_sdk_2.s for sections that could not yet be fully separated or represented mixed object content.
Split out AXDriver from the code section at the bottom of Sysdolphin files, with the PR body noting AXDriver is technically only one of the C file names.
Split psdisp out from between other ps code.
Split up remaining CW MSL C code, including moving content into files such as strng.s, strtoul.s, printf.s, console_io.s, fio.s, wchar_io.s, rand.s, and math.s.
Updated ldscript.lcf with 54 added and 7 deleted lines to reflect the new assembly file layout and preserve linking order.

The diff excerpt shows newly added asm/AX.s using .include "macros.inc", .section .text, global labels such as AXInitEx, __AXGetStackHead, and __AXAuxInit, and many still-unnamed address-based func_ and lbl_ symbols.
smart_moves: Split by linker-map/object-file evidence where possible instead of leaving all SDK and Sysdolphin code in monolithic address blobs.

Kept large residual files for mixed .o regions rather than forcing uncertain splits; the PR body explicitly says some large sections remained because of mixed .o files according to the linker map or because CARD and AX were not fully split yet.
Renamed files to avoid linker partial filename matching hazards, choosing dsp_dbg instead of dsp_debug and strng instead of string.
Preserved matchability by pairing asm file movement with ldscript.lcf updates rather than treating file splitting as a purely cosmetic rename.
Used subsystem/object-like filenames such as AX, CARD, GXFifo, GXInit, dvdlow, dsp_task, and jpegdec to make later C decompilation easier to search and attribute.
decomp_lessons: For early decomp organization work, splitting giant asm blobs into map-informed object/module files can materially improve searchability and future ownership even before functions are converted to C.
Do not assume the apparent source filename can always be used verbatim in this build system; filename collisions and partial object-name matching can change link placement.
When linker-map evidence indicates mixed object files, preserve a coarse residual split instead of pretending the boundaries are known.
Large SDK subsystems such as CARD and AX may need staged splitting: first isolate the subsystem into a named asm file, then refine object boundaries in later work.
Any asm file split must be reflected in the linker script; otherwise file organization can silently break matching order.
Address-based symbols such as func_80358B60 and lbl_804A8D78 are acceptable temporary anchors when a PR's purpose is module splitting rather than symbol recovery.
naming_conventions: Use Dolphin SDK subsystem/object-like names for asm files when supported by the linker map, e.g. AX.s, CARD.s, GXFifo.s, GXInit.s, dvdlow.s, dsp_task.s, jpegdec.s.
Avoid object filenames that are substrings of other object filenames when the linker partially matches names.
Use dsp_dbg rather than dsp_debug to avoid the documented linker filename-matching issue.
Use strng rather than string for MSL string code to avoid collision or ordering problems with names such as cstring.o/string.o.
Keep residual unknown or mixed sections in address-named files such as code_803A3920.s and code_8038FAA8.s when stronger names are not available.
Continue using func_803xxxxx and lbl_80xxxxxx placeholders inside split asm files when actual function or data names are not yet known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed bulk asm file splitting without changing the assembly body semantics, moving code from monolithic files into smaller files while retaining labels, addresses, and macros.inc includes.
Used ldscript.lcf changes to encode the new object/file sequence needed for matching.
Relied on linker-map object boundaries where available, but retained large files for mixed .o regions.
Worked around linker partial filename matching by selecting non-colliding shortened filenames.
Kept .section .text declarations and global labels in newly split files, as shown in the AX.s excerpt.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The only rationale evidence is from the PR body and changed-file list; no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert the split AX.s or AXDriver.s assembly into C.
Find later PRs that refine CARD or AX object boundaries after this coarse split.
Search for uses of dsp_dbg and strng in linker script history to understand filename collision workarounds.
Find PRs touching ldscript.lcf after PR #24 to see whether the split ordering was further corrected.
Search for later decomp work in psdisp, jpegdec, dvdlow, GXFifo, and GXInit files.
